articles.title: Blue Jays unveil statue outside Rogers Centre honouring World Series championships
articles.summary: A statue honouring the 1992 and 1993 World Series champion Toronto Blue Jays has been unveiled outside of Rogers Centre. It shows right-fielder Joe Carter mid-jump as he rounds the bases following his walk-off home run to win the 1993 World Series. The statue is located at the southeast corner of the ballpark, outside Gate […]

articles.title: Woman killed in North York stabbing, police say
articles.summary: A woman was killed during a stabbing in North York on Saturday afternoon, according to Toronto police. Officers were called to a residential building near Fairview Mall Drive and Don Mills Road just after 2:30 p.m. and found a woman with stab wounds inside the building. Police say the woman was pronounced dead at the […]

articles.title: 2 charged in Toronto police distraction theft probe after grandmother, child allegedly targeted
articles.summary: Toronto police officers allege the incident happened on the morning of May 18 near Culford Road and Falstaff Avenue.

articles.title: Severe thunderstorm watches, earlier tornado warnings across Greater Toronto Area dropped
articles.summary: Environment and Climate Change Canada meteorologists say the Greater Toronto Area could also see hail and up to 40 mm. of rain.

articles.title: Peel police identify 17-year-old brothers as suspects in fatal Brampton shooting
articles.summary: Peel police have identified two teenage brothers as suspects in a fatal shooting in Brampton last week.

articles.title: Police identify 17-year-old brothers as suspects in fatal Brampton shooting
articles.summary: Peel Regional Police officers say the incident happened in Brampton near Edenbrook Hill Drive and Earlsbridge Boulevard on July 5, 2026.

articles.title: Ajax man charged with sexual assault in Whitby
articles.summary: A 33-year-old Ajax man is facing several charges after a woman claims she was sexually assaulted in Whitby. Police in Durham Region say that just after 10 p.m. on July 13, the woman was leaving Iroquois Beach Park and was walking along the sand path that runs north-west toward Gordon Street when she was forced […]

articles.title: Metrolinx cleaning chemical spill in east-end creek related to Scarborough subway extension work
articles.summary: City says it was notified of spill on July 8. Metrolinx says the spill is contained and a specialized team is working to remove "polymer-based slurry" from creek.

articles.title: Mosquitoes in Toronto, York and Halton test positive for West Nile virus
articles.summary: A batch of mosquitoes in Toronto has tested positive for West Nile virus, Toronto Public Health says.
